mechanism_narrative: Higher retail sales (RSAFS) indicate robust consumer spending, which directly feeds into corporate revenues and earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: The 20-day forward return following months with top-quartile RSAFS growth is not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) above 6% signals a weakening labor market and reduced consumer spending power, which negatively impacts corporate earnings and depresses S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In the 12 months following UNRATE > 6%, SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is at a very low level (below 0.25%) and the inflation rate (CPIAUCSL) begins to rise, real interest rates fall, but the market anticipates eventual tightening, leading to negative forward returns over the medium term as uncertainty about monetary policy increases.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS < 0.25 AND CPIAUCSL > previous month value
regime_hint: low nominal rate environment with rising inflation
failure_condition: The interaction term (FEDFUNDS * CPIAUCSL change) does not have a statistically significant negative coefficient (p<0.05) in a regression on 60-day forward returns over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of high unemployment (UNRATE > 6.5%) and low industrial production (INDPRO < 100), an increase in payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals an imminent recovery in the labor market and aggregate demand, which is rewarded by positive S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days as recession fears dissipate.
regime_hint: recession environment with early recovery signs
failure_condition: In the two years following the trigger, the correlation between monthly PAYEMS change and subsequent 20-day returns is not positive (zero or negative).

mechanism_narrative: A liftoff from the zero lower bound (FEDFUNDS crossing above 0.25%) signals the beginning of a tightening cycle, which typically leads to downward repricing of equity risk premiums and lower S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week as the market adjusts to higher discount rates.
failure_condition: In the 6 months following the first FEDFUNDS rise above 0.25%, the average 5-day forward return is positive or not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) reflects strengthening manufacturing activity, which is a leading indicator of corporate profits and economic expansion, thus supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the longer horizon of a quarter as the supply side gains momentum.
failure_condition: The 60-day forward return following months with INDPRO growth in the top tertile is not significantly positive (p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The interaction between housing starts (HOUST) and unemployment rate (UNRATE) provides a composite signal of housing market health and labor market slack: when housing starts rise and unemployment falls, it indicates durable demand improvement, boosting equity valuations over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal (HOUST growth minus UNRATE change) does not have a positive Spearman correlation with 60-day forward returns over a 10-year span.

mechanism_narrative: In a recovery environment characterized by positive GDP growth (GDPC1 YoY > 0) and still-elevated unemployment (UNRATE > 6%), an increase in housing starts (HOUST) signals pent-up demand and consumer confidence, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the near term as cyclical stocks benefit.
failure_condition: When trigger is active, the subsequent 20-day returns following a rise in HOUST are not positive in at least 60% of cases.
